ocr.text: The following table presents an analysis of miRNA gene expression related to key genetic markers and their associated clinical
implications. Specifically, it focuses on the miR-17 gene, which has been implicated in various oncogenic processes. The data
reveals a notable nucleotide change (Nt Change) of C/T, underscoring the potential for this variant to influence gene regulation
and, consequently, disease susceptibility. In examining allele frequencies, we observe a frequency of 0.012, which indicates the

rarity of this particular variant in the studied population. 

Furthermore, the selected target genes associated with miR-17 include
critical tumor suppressors such as BRCA1, PTEN, p53, and ATM, all of which are pivotal in maintaining genomic stability and
preventing tumorigenesis. The table also includes metrics related to the antibody response, with a positive cutoff reference value
set at 20 EU. Notably, the median value in Crohn's Disease (CD) is reported as 26.9, with a range spanning from 0 to 415.2,
highlighting the variability in immune response among individuals diagnosed with CD. This data illustrates the intricate
relationship between miRNA expression and genetic predisposition to disease, warranting further investigation into their
clinical applications.
miRNA genes  Nt Change  Location  Allele frequency  Selected target genes (examples)*
miR -17  C/T  Pri-miRNA  0.012  BRCA1, PTEN, p53 and ATM
hsa-mir-191  C/T  Pri-miRNA  0.006  IGF1R, FANCC, FANCF, FANCD2, MLH3
C/G  pri-miRNA  0.012
C/A  Pre-miRNA  0.006
hsa-mir-29b-2  C/T  pri-miRNA  0.042  DNMT3A, IGFI, CCND2, BCL2
A/T  pre-miRNA  0.021  DNMT3A, IGFI, CCND2, BCL2
hsa-mir-188  T/C  pri-miRNA  0.006  CCND2, XRCC2, IGFI, E2F
Antibody  Positive cutoff reference value  Median value in CD, Range  Percent positive in CD
